answer:
Well, when it comes to movies and books, I really gravitate towards fantasy and sci-fi. I think what draws me in is the sheer creativity and escapism they offer. I mean, who wouldn't want to explore magical worlds or travel through time? They allow us to break free from reality, which is something I find really refreshing, especially after a long week at school.

One story that stands out for me is "The Hobbit" by J.R.R. Tolkien. It's not just the adventure of Bilbo Baggins; it's also about personal growth and bravery. I remember feeling so connected to Bilbo as he transformed from this timid hobbit into a courageous hero. It kind of mirrors how we all face challenges and discover our strengths, even when we don't initially see them in ourselves. 

Also, shows like "Stranger Things" capture my attention because of the mix of nostalgia and thrilling mystery. The characters feel so real, and the plot keeps you guessing. There's something about the blend of friendship and overcoming obstacles that really resonates with me.

So, yeah, I guess I enjoy stories that transport me somewhere extraordinary while still touching on universal themes that anyone can relate to. They inspire me to think differently and appreciate the magic in everyday life, which I think is just so important, especially as I'm stepping into this new phase of adulthood.
title: What type of movies, shows, or books do you prefer? Why do you enjoy this style? Which story stands out and why?
